So you got to have them perpetually interested in you and what you're up to and what you're thinking and what your opinions are and what is she going to say next and you know all of that and then they won't go away. Even if the thought occurs to them they won't go away. Okay. Here's some examples of strategies to be talked about. Number one, do out of ordinary things. Vacationing in China is much more interesting than vacationing in Dubuque. Now Matt happens to have other reasons to go to China but since he has reasons to go to China he is able to use it and uses it to the hilt because most people don't go to China so that immediately is a very interesting thing. And so every year for two weeks while Matt's in China what everybody's talking about is Matt's in China. What's he doing over there? What's he going to bring back? Everybody's emailing each other. Matt's in China. I don't talk to him much. I don't work with him directly. I obviously don't get any email. I somehow know when he's in China. Why? Because people are telling me. Matt's in China again for two whole weeks. If he was in LA so what? Yannick has this adventurer thing. He's forking over the money to be one of the first 100 that Richard Branson shoots up in space in a rocket. I don't know when that's going to happen but it's not next week so it's relatively cheap money because you're probably going to get to talk about it for two or three years before it actually happens.
